message:DataSet.SUGGESTED_CITATION: International Monetary Fund. World Economic Outlook (WEO), https://data.imf.org/en/datasets/IMF.RES:WEO. Accessed on [current date].
message:DataSet.KEYWORDS_DATASET: Demographics, Real sector, Price indexes, Gross domestic product, National accounts, Labor markets, Unemployment, International trade, Export prices, Import prices, International reserves, Monetary aggregates, Consumer prices, Exchange rates, External position, Financial account, Balance of payments, Capital account, Current account, External debt, International investment position, Public sector and general government, Debt service, Fiscal account
message:DataSet.FULL_DESCRIPTION: The World Economic Outlook (WEO) database contains selected macroeconomic data series from the statistical appendix of the World Economic Outlook report, which presents the IMF staff's analysis and projections of economic developments at the global level, in major country groups and in many individual countries. The WEO is released in April and September/October each year. Use this database to find data on national accounts, gross domestic product (GDP), inflation, unemployment rates, balance of payments, fiscal indicators, trade for countries and country groups (aggregates), and commodity prices whose data are reported by the IMF. Data are available from 1980 to the present, and projections are given for the next two years. Additionally, medium-term projections are available for selected indicators. For some countries, data are incomplete or unavailable for certain years.
message:DataSet.LICENSE: © International Monetary Fund Copyright. All Rights Reserved. https://www.imf.org/external/terms.htm

Group.SERIES_NAME: Soybean meal, Unit prices, US dollars per metric tonne
Imports of goods and services, US dollar
Natural gas, Commodity price index
Hard sawnwood, Dark Red Meranti, Unit prices, US dollars per cubic meter

Group.METHODOLOGY_NOTES: Historical consumer price index (CPI) and inflation are measured by Angola's national CPI (IPCN) from January 2015 and by the Luanda province CPI up to December 2014. Although the base year is reported as 2010, we are currently using December 2010 (=100) as the base month.

Group.SERIES_NAME: Soybeans oil, Unit prices, US dollars per metric tonne
Metal, Commodity price index
Structural balance, General government, Percent
Agricultural raw materials, Commodity price index
Swine, Unit prices, US cents per pound
Gross domestic product (GDP), Constant prices, Percent change
External debt, US dollar
Financial account balance (assets less liabilities), US dollar

Group.METHODOLOGY_NOTES: Data from 2018 onwards follows the GFSM2014 methodology. Data prior to 2018 follows the GFSM2001 methodology. Basis of recording: Angola is implementing an in-between methodology for its revenues and expenditure: cash basis for its revenues and accrual basis for the expenditures. There is accordingly a lag in recording for the former, and an accrual adjustment is implemented for revenues. General government composition: Central Government and provincial governments.
Group.BASIS_OF_PROJECTIONS: Oil revenue: effective tax rate approach, based on WEO oil price and oil production from authorities; non-oil revenue: relies on non-oil output as key taxable base; expenditure: current year based on the budget, medium term based on desk's assumptions.

Group.SERIES_NAME: Iron ore, Unit prices, US dollars per metric tonne

Group.TRADE_SECTOR_OIL_COVERAGE: Primary or unrefined products; Secondary or refined products; Other
Group.METHODOLOGY_NOTES: Historical data source: National Bank of Angola. Methodology used to derive volumes: Explicit volume numbers on exports are used (e.g. barrels exported). Import volumes are derived using an estimated unit value index. Trade system: General trade. Valuation of exports: Free on board (FOB). Valuation of imports: Cost, insurance, freight (CIF).

Historical consumer price index (CPI) and inflation are measured by Angola's national CPI (IPCN) from January 2015 and by the Luanda province CPI up to December 2014. Although the base year is reported as 2010, we are currently using December 2010 (=100) as the base month.

Group.SERIES_NAME: Olive oil, Unit prices, US dollars per metric tonne
Beverages, Commodity price index
Uranium, Unit prices, US dollars per pound
Exports of goods and services, US dollar

Group.METHODOLOGY_NOTES: Historical data source: National Bank of Angola. Methodology used to derive volumes: Explicit volume numbers on exports are used (e.g. barrels exported). Import volumes are derived using an estimated unit value index. Trade system: General trade. Valuation of exports: Free on board (FOB). Valuation of imports: Cost, insurance, freight (CIF).
Group.TRADE_SECTOR_OIL_COVERAGE: Primary or unrefined products; Secondary or refined products; Other

Group.SERIES_NAME: External debt: total debt service, amortization, Percent of GDP
Change in reserve assets, Net (assets minus liabilities), US dollar
Groundnuts, Unit prices, US dollars per metric tonne

Group.METHODOLOGY_NOTES: Historical data source: Ministry of Planning. Starting from the year 2002, historical National Accounts data are provided by Angola's National Institute of Statistics (INE) and the composition of aggregate demand was revised taking into account updated information from the authorities National Accounts statistics. Data for prior years are based on different sources and/or estimated by IMF staff, including using a ratio slicing method in order to avoid breaks in the data. The Bureau of Statistics, Instituto Nacional De EstatÍsticas, has published rebased national accounts in May 2024. The base year was revised from 2002 to 2015 to reflect changes in the economic structure over that period. As a result, 2023 nominal GDP increased by 13.1 percent. The main sector that was upwardly revised is agriculture.

Group.SERIES_NAME: Coffee, other mild Arabica, Unit prices, US cents per pound
APSP crude oil, Unit prices, US dollars per barrel
Imports of goods and services, Volume, Cost insurance freight (CIF), Percent change
Rice, Thailand, Unit prices, US dollars per metric tonne
Employed persons, Persons for countries / Index for country groups
Expenditure, General government, Domestic currency

Group.METHODOLOGY_NOTES: Data from 2018 onwards follows the GFSM2014 methodology. Data prior to 2018 follows the GFSM2001 methodology. Basis of recording: Angola is implementing an in-between methodology for its revenues and expenditure: cash basis for its revenues and accrual basis for the expenditures. There is accordingly a lag in recording for the former, and an accrual adjustment is implemented for revenues. General government composition: Central Government and provincial governments.
Group.BASIS_OF_PROJECTIONS: Oil revenue: effective tax rate approach, based on WEO oil price and oil production from authorities; non-oil revenue: relies on non-oil output as key taxable base; expenditure: current year based on the budget, medium term based on desk's assumptions.

Group.METHODOLOGY_NOTES: Historical data source: National Bank of Angola. Methodology used to derive volumes: Explicit volume numbers on exports are used (e.g. barrels exported). Import volumes are derived using an estimated unit value index. Trade system: General trade. Valuation of exports: Free on board (FOB). Valuation of imports: Cost, insurance, freight (CIF).
Group.TRADE_SECTOR_OIL_COVERAGE: Primary or unrefined products; Secondary or refined products; Other

Group.SERIES_NAME: Sunflower oil, Export price, US dollars per metric tonne
Meat, Commodity price index
Terms of trade of goods, percent change
Imports of goods, Volume, Cost insurance freight (CIF), Percent change
Gross domestic product (GDP), Current prices, Fiscal year, Domestic currency

Group.BASIS_OF_PROJECTIONS: Oil revenue: effective tax rate approach, based on WEO oil price and oil production from authorities; non-oil revenue: relies on non-oil output as key taxable base; expenditure: current year based on the budget, medium term based on desk's assumptions.
Group.METHODOLOGY_NOTES: Data from 2018 onwards follows the GFSM2014 methodology. Data prior to 2018 follows the GFSM2001 methodology. Basis of recording: Angola is implementing an in-between methodology for its revenues and expenditure: cash basis for its revenues and accrual basis for the expenditures. There is accordingly a lag in recording for the former, and an accrual adjustment is implemented for revenues. General government composition: Central Government and provincial governments.

Historical data source: Ministry of Planning. Starting from the year 2002, historical National Accounts data are provided by Angola's National Institute of Statistics (INE) and the composition of aggregate demand was revised taking into account updated information from the authorities National Accounts statistics. Data for prior years are based on different sources and/or estimated by IMF staff, including using a ratio slicing method in order to avoid breaks in the data. The Bureau of Statistics, Instituto Nacional De EstatÍsticas, has published rebased national accounts in May 2024. The base year was revised from 2002 to 2015 to reflect changes in the economic structure over that period. As a result, 2023 nominal GDP increased by 13.1 percent. The main sector that was upwardly revised is agriculture.

Revenue, General government, Domestic currency

Coal, Commodity price index

Group.METHODOLOGY_NOTES: Data from 2018 onwards follows the GFSM2014 methodology. Data prior to 2018 follows the GFSM2001 methodology. Basis of recording: Angola is implementing an in-between methodology for its revenues and expenditure: cash basis for its revenues and accrual basis for the expenditures. There is accordingly a lag in recording for the former, and an accrual adjustment is implemented for revenues. General government composition: Central Government and provincial governments.

Fish, Export price, US dollars per kilogram
External debt, Percent of exports of goods and services
Shrimp, Unit prices, US dollars per kilogram
Revenue, General government, Percent of GDP
Gross domestic product (GDP), Purchasing power parity (PPP) international dollar, percent of world GDP, Percent, ICP benchmarks 2017-2021

Group.METHODOLOGY_NOTES: Historical data source: Ministry of Planning. Starting from the year 2002, historical National Accounts data are provided by Angola's National Institute of Statistics (INE) and the composition of aggregate demand was revised taking into account updated information from the authorities National Accounts statistics. Data for prior years are based on different sources and/or estimated by IMF staff, including using a ratio slicing method in order to avoid breaks in the data. The Bureau of Statistics, Instituto Nacional De EstatÍsticas, has published rebased national accounts in May 2024. The base year was revised from 2002 to 2015 to reflect changes in the economic structure over that period. As a result, 2023 nominal GDP increased by 13.1 percent. The main sector that was upwardly revised is agriculture.

Starting from the year 2002, historical National Accounts data are provided by Angola's National Institute of Statistics (INE) and the composition of aggregate demand was revised taking into account updated information from the authorities National Accounts statistics. Data for prior years are based on different sources and/or estimated by IMF staff, including using a ratio slicing method in order to avoid breaks in the data. The Bureau of Statistics, Instituto Nacional De EstatÍsticas, has published rebased national accounts in May 2024. The base year was revised from 2002 to 2015 to reflect changes in the economic structure over that period. As a result, 2023 nominal GDP increased by 13.1 percent. The main sector that was upwardly revised is agriculture.

Group.METHODOLOGY_NOTES: Historical data source: Ministry of Planning. Starting from the year 2002, historical National Accounts data are provided by Angola's National Institute of Statistics (INE) and the composition of aggregate demand was revised taking into account updated information from the authorities National Accounts statistics. Data for prior years are based on different sources and/or estimated by IMF staff, including using a ratio slicing method in order to avoid breaks in the data. The Bureau of Statistics, Instituto Nacional De EstatÍsticas, has published rebased national accounts in May 2024. The base year was revised from 2002 to 2015 to reflect changes in the economic structure over that period. As a result, 2023 nominal GDP increased by 13.1 percent. The main sector that was upwardly revised is agriculture.

Historical data source: Ministry of Planning. Starting from the year 2002, historical National Accounts data are provided by Angola's National Institute of Statistics (INE) and the composition of aggregate demand was revised taking into account updated information from the authorities National Accounts statistics. Data for prior years are based on different sources and/or estimated by IMF staff, including using a ratio slicing method in order to avoid breaks in the data. The Bureau of Statistics, Instituto Nacional De EstatÍsticas, has published rebased national accounts in May 2024. The base year was revised from 2002 to 2015 to reflect changes in the economic structure over that period. As a result, 2023 nominal GDP increased by 13.1 percent. The main sector that was upwardly revised is agriculture.

Group.METHODOLOGY_NOTES: Historical data source: Ministry of Planning. Starting from the year 2002, historical National Accounts data are provided by Angola's National Institute of Statistics (INE) and the composition of aggregate demand was revised taking into account updated information from the authorities National Accounts statistics. Data for prior years are based on different sources and/or estimated by IMF staff, including using a ratio slicing method in order to avoid breaks in the data. The Bureau of Statistics, Instituto Nacional De EstatÍsticas, has published rebased national accounts in May 2024. The base year was revised from 2002 to 2015 to reflect changes in the economic structure over that period. As a result, 2023 nominal GDP increased by 13.1 percent. The main sector that was upwardly revised is agriculture.

The Bureau of Statistics, Instituto Nacional De EstatÍsticas, has published rebased national accounts in May 2024. The base year was revised from 2002 to 2015 to reflect changes in the economic structure over that period. As a result, 2023 nominal GDP increased by 13.1 percent. Data for prior years are based on different sources and/or estimated by IMF staff, including using a ratio slicing method in order to avoid breaks in the data. The Bureau of Statistics, Instituto Nacional De EstatÍsticas, has published rebased national accounts in May 2024. The base year was revised from 2002 to 2015 to reflect changes in the economic structure over that period. As a result, 2023 nominal GDP increased by 13.1 percent. The main sector that was upwardly revised is agriculture.

Basis of recording: Angola is implementing an in-between methodology for its revenues and expenditure: cash basis for its revenues and accrual basis for the expenditures. There is accordingly a lag in recording for the former, and an accrual adjustment is implemented for revenues. General government composition: Central Government and provincial governments.

Group.METHODOLOGY_NOTES: Historical consumer price index (CPI) and inflation are measured by Angola's national CPI (IPCN) from January 2015 and by the Luanda province CPI up to December 2014. Although the base year is reported as 2010, we are currently using December 2010 (=100) as the base month.

Data from 2018 onwards follows the GFSM2014 methodology. Data prior to 2018 follows the GFSM2001 methodology. Basis of recording: Angola is implementing an in-between methodology for its revenues and expenditure: cash basis for its revenues and accrual basis for the expenditures. There is accordingly a lag in recording for the former, and an accrual adjustment is implemented for revenues. General government composition: Central Government and provincial governments.

Group.METHODOLOGY_NOTES: Historical data source: Ministry of Planning. Starting from the year 2002, historical National Accounts data are provided by Angola's National Institute of Statistics (INE) and the composition of aggregate demand was revised taking into account updated information from the authorities National Accounts statistics. Data for prior years are based on different sources and/or estimated by IMF staff, including using a ratio slicing method in order to avoid breaks in the data. The Bureau of Statistics, Instituto Nacional De EstatÍsticas, has published rebased national accounts in May 2024. The base year was revised from 2002 to 2015 to reflect changes in the economic structure over that period. As a result, 2023 nominal GDP increased by 13.1 percent. The main sector that was upwardly revised is agriculture.

Starting from the year 2002, historical National Accounts data are provided by Angola's National Institute of Statistics (INE) and the composition of aggregate demand was revised taking into account updated information from the authorities National Accounts statistics. Data for prior years are based on different sources and/or estimated by IMF staff, including using a ratio slicing method in order to avoid breaks in the data. The Bureau of Statistics, Instituto Nacional De EstatÍsticas, has published rebased national accounts in May 2024. The base year was revised from 2002 to 2015 to reflect changes in the economic structure over that period. As a result, 2023 nominal GDP increased by 13.1 percent. The main sector that was upwardly revised is agriculture.
